In order to illustrate the general applicability of this catalytic
system, a variety of diene-tethered alkynyl halides were synthe-
Table 2: Optimization of solvent and temperature for the [IrCl(cod)]2/

and 1g (Table 3). Alcohol 8 was prepared in two steps from
tiglic aldehyde (7) as described by Gilbertson [47], and
dienynes 9 and 11 could be synthesized from 8 (Scheme 3).
Deprotonation of 8 with sodium hydride, followed by trapping
with propargyl bromide provided 9 in 60% yield, and a
Mitsunobu reaction between 8 and sulfonamide 10 (prepared as

and 11 provided diene-tethered alkynyl halides 1c (57%) and 1e
